title: anthemis rosea subsp. carnea
intro: Anthemis rosea subsp. carnea, also known as pink chamomile or white daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family. It is commonly used in herbal medicine for its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in teas, salves, and infusions, which have been employed to treat a variety of health issues. The medicinal properties of Anthemis rosea subsp. carnea include its ability to calm digestive issues, reduce anxiety and insomnia, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, can be used to promote relaxation, soothe skin irritations, and support overall well-being.
